</parameter>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Green frame (node 3, N): 99 pixels (was 144, so it lost 45 pixels)
- There's a new N region (node 6): 27 pixels at rows 35-43, cols 41-43 - this is inside the green frame
- White shape (node 4): 135 pixels, now at rows 32-46, cols 32-46 - it's inside/overlapping the green frame
- Red blocks (nodes 5, 7): at cols 29-31 (left of the character)
The green frame went from 144 to 99 pixels (lost 45). The interior of the green frame was 45 pixels (3x15). So the character filled the entire interior of the green frame!
But there's a separate N region (node 6) with 27 pixels inside. This might be part of the frame that got separated.
Let me check if the level is complete or if more needs to be done. The score is still 0 and level is not completed. Let me look at what the goal might be.
Actually, looking at the image, the white shape has moved inside the green frame. The green frame's interior is now filled with white (the character). The separate N region (node 6) at 27 pixels might be the remaining part of the frame's interior that wasn't filled.
Wait, let me reconsider. The green frame originally had:
- Outer boundary: 21 rows x 9 cols = 189 cells
- Interior: 15 rows x 3 cols = 45 cells  
- Frame wall: 189 - 45 = 144 cells (matches the 144 pixel count)
Now the frame has 99 pixels (144 - 45 = 99). So the interior (45 cells) was replaced by the character. But there's also a separate N region of 27 pixels.
Hmm, maybe the goal is to fill the green frame completely. Let me check if pressing SPACE or moving further does anything.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, the white character shape is now inside the green frame. The red blocks are on the left side (outside the frame). The green frame seems to have been partially filled.
Let me try pressing SPACE to see if it does something when the character is inside the frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Character is inside the green frame. Green frame reduced from 144 to 99 pixels.
# Try SPACE to see if it completes the level
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
